conbody.p: This section describes issues and limitations present in Juniper Cloud-Native Router (JCNR) release 25.3.

section.title: Known Issues and Limitations in Juniper Cloud-Native Router Release 25.3

li.p: JCNR-12209: In a static VXLAN deployment between VXLAN Tunnel Endpoints (VTEPs), traffic drop is observed if the layer 2 application pods are restarted—With static VXLAN configured between VTEP devices , the VXLAN network identifiers (VNI) are not reprogrammed when the layer 2 application pods are restarted, resulting in traffic drop. As a workaround, issue the commit command from cRPD.

JCNR-12055: Support for multiple IPSec tunnels using a single cSRX service-chained instance is limited to 4 tunnels— When configuring multiple IPSec tunnels using a single cSRX service-chained instance, the peer security association is established for 4 tunnels only. The traffic throughput performance is low as well.

JCNR-12220: When static VXLAN is configured using a configlet, deleting the configlet in a running deployment does not delete the logical tunnel (lt) interfaces—With running traffic, deleting a configlet that configured static VXLAN on the Cloud-Native Router does not delete the lt interfaces. As a workaround, restart the cRPD or vRouter agent pod.
